subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, OECD, World Bank, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Chilean official (INE, Banco Central de Chile) + Chilean/Spanish-language press.
Politics: two failed constitutional rewrites (2022/2023) ended the reform cycle; the Dec-2025 runoff produced an orderly transfer to a security-focused right-wing government; institutions intact.
Standout drags: rising organized crime, a seismic/megadrought climate tail, and a steep long-term demographic decline. Source-lean: moderate Anglophone; Spanish-language press under-read.

economic.notes: Prudent macro management (a structural-balance rule, a highly credible central bank) and copper/lithium strength.

fiscal_state.notes: Low/sustainable debt under a structural-balance rule plus sovereign funds; broadly balanced target by 2027; sovereign-fund depletion the watch-item. (IMF Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: The Banco Central de Chile is among the region's most credible inflation-targeters, with a flexible FX. (BCCh; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The peso is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Converged to ~2.4%, around the 3% target. (BCCh; INE)

banking_stability.notes: Sound, well-capitalised, withstands severe stress; diminished financial depth noted. (IMF FSSA; BCCh)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: World #1 copper and huge lithium reserves; a 2025 national lithium strategy is more state-centric (may deter some FDI); China-concentration and paradox-of-plenty risk mitigated by strong institutions. (USGS; NRGI)

institutional.notes: Strong by regional standards (independent courts, low corruption, credible statistics), with a multi-year decline trend and press concentration.

rule_of_law.notes: Strong regionally, independent courts, low corruption (TI CPI ~63), but a multi-year 'decliner' from its peak. (TI 2025; WJP 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: INE/BCCh are credible and independently cited. (IMF; INE)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Professional and meritocratic, with strong fiscal/monetary/electoral administration. (IMF; WJP)

press_freedom.notes: Free but RSF ~#69 (a sharp 2025 setback) with a heavy media-ownership duopoly. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: Resilient institutions and an orderly transfer, against rising organized crime and a steep long-term demographic decline.

political_stability.notes: Resilient institutions; an orderly Dec-2025 election with a peaceful transfer; two failed constitutional rewrites ended the reform churn, with crime now the top voter issue. (AS/COA)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong, rated 'Free,' with a mild watch on the incoming hardline security posture. (Freedom House; V-Dem)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful residency exists but is visibly tightening after a large 2021-24 migration wave (a 2024 reform, longer naturalization, stricter asylum). (MPI; OECD)

social_cohesion.notes: A 2019-unrest legacy, high inequality, and rising organized crime (Tren de Aragua), with record kidnappings. (InSight Crime)

demographics.notes: TFR ~1.1 (region's lowest); population peaks ~2035 then declines - a real long-term drag. (INE; UN)

geopolitical.notes: A stable, pragmatic US-aligned partner with no external conflict and an open capital account.

alliance_reliability.notes: A stable, pragmatic, close US ally and Pacific Alliance core, balancing China dependence. (State Dept)

conflict_involvement.notes: No external conflict; internal Mapuche/Araucania tension under a repeated state of emergency. (MercoPress)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open capital account, no controls, unsanctioned. (IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Best-in-region infrastructure and a seismic-aware insurance market, against a seismic/megadrought/wildfire tail and rising crime.

climate.notes: A seismic mega-hazard plus a 2010-22 megadrought/central water stress and deadly 2024 Valparaiso fires. (USGS; NASA EO)

healthcare.notes: Mixed Fonasa/Isapre with decent access/outcomes; Isapre solvency strain. (PAHO; IMF)

infrastructure.notes: Best-in-region ports, telecom, electrification, and roads. (trade.gov)

crime_safety.notes: Homicide ~6/100k (low globally but up), with an organized-crime/kidnapping surge the new concern. (InSight Crime)

disaster_insurance.notes: A seismic-aware market with decent earthquake penetration and mandatory mortgage cover. (market context)

summary: Among the most prudently managed economies in its region - a structural-balance fiscal rule, a highly credible central bank, sound banks, and world-leading copper/lithium endowments managed within strong institutions. The drags are rising organized crime (a new security politics), a tightening migration stance, a seismic/megadrought/wildfire climate tail, press-ownership concentration, and a steep long-term demographic decline. Institutions proved resilient through two failed constitutional rewrites and an orderly 2025 transfer of power.
